betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Material Safety§f

§aProtect yourself from radiation, pyrophoric metals, hydroreactive elements, and more.§f

Many HBM materials are hazardous. Here is what you need to know:

§6Radiation§f
Ionizing radiation is the deadliest hazard. Your dose accumulates without decreasing naturally. At 1000 RAD you die.
§b• Wear hazmat armor§f for base resistance
§b• Use Rad-X§f (+0.2 resistance) for early-game protection
§b• Stand on a §bdecontaminator§f§f to remove 10 RAD/s
§b• Armor mods (cladding)§f boost protection. The §bLiquidator Suit§f is the best pre-oil armor.

§6Pyrophoric§f
These materials ignite when held. Use §bTungsten Reachers§f to handle them safely — they also reduce radiation from held items.

§6Hydroreactive§f
Explodes on contact with water. Do not carry these items in rain or near water. No mitigation beyond avoidance.

§6Flammable / Explosive§f
Ignites in lava or fire. Combining flammable items with pyrophoric materials without reachers is especially dangerous.

§6Blinding§f
Schrabidium and schrabidic compounds cause blindness while held. It fades 5 seconds after removal. The FEL with visible-light crystal causes permanent blindness (cure: milk or iodine pill).

§6Digamma§f
Extremely rare radiation from balefire bombs and digamma items. Treat it like normal radiation but with no early-game mitigation.
